page.content: The following data table presents a comparative analysis of psychological measures related to recovery outcomes among participants, focusing specifically on the Fear-Avoidance Beliefs Questionnaire-Physical Activity (FABQ-PA) scores. With a sample size of 14 individuals categorized as "Recovered" and 97 classified as "Not Recovered," the results indicate a significant disparity between the two groups. The mean FABQ-PA score for those who have recovered was notably lower at 5.1 ± 5.7, compared to 12.5 ± 6.7 for participants who did not recover. This difference is statistically significant, as evidenced by a p-value of <.001, highlighting the role of psychological factors in recovery trajectories. Additionally, the table includes data on genotype distribution, specifically the Rs10954213 allele variants, which may further elucidate the biological underpinnings associated with recovery outcomes. The information provided not only underscores the importance of psychological measures in evaluating recovery but also suggests potential avenues for further investigation into the interplay between genetic factors and mental health in rehabilitation contexts.
